commonIssues:
Thousands of ration cards are being cancelled or flagged due to strict new eligibility criteria, including owning a four-wheeler, paying income tax, owning property in specific areas, or having a high power consumption (>2kW).

How to fix:

  • Check your status on the NFS Delhi portal (nfs.delhigovt.nic.in).
  • If flagged for high power consumption but you live in a multi-family house with a single meter, submit an affidavit and electricity bills to your Circle Office for re-verification.

Mandatory biometric authentication at Point of Sale (PoS) devices frequently fails, particularly for elderly people or manual labourers whose fingerprints do not match, causing them to lose their monthly ration.

How to fix:

  • Request the dealer to use the OTP-based authentication sent to the mobile number registered with your Aadhaar card.
  • If fingerprints fail consistently, update your biometrics at the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra or apply to the Food Department for a 'Nominee' authorization to collect ration on your behalf.

Many genuine beneficiaries are facing cancellation notices due to data mismatches, such as ancestral agricultural land records showing them as wealthy landowners, or errors in vehicle ownership records.

How to fix:

  • Collect physical proof (e.g., land revenue records showing the land is not yours, or RTO certificates proving you do not own a 4-wheeler).
  • Visit the Food & Civil Supplies office in your district with these documents to refute the mismatch flagged by the system.

There are difficulties in updating family details, specifically adding newborn babies or removing family members who have passed away or moved out, leading to issues with allocated ration amounts.

How to fix:

  • Login to the e-District Delhi portal and apply for 'Modification of Ration Card' or used the 'Add member' facility.
  • Upload the Birth Certificate (for newborns) or Death Certificate (for removal) and follow up if the change is not reflected within 30 days.

Beneficiaries who have not completed e-KYC or have not collected ration for over six months (known as 'silent' beneficiaries) are having their cards blocked.

How to fix:

  • Immediately visit your designated Fair Price Shop (FPS) to check the status of your card.
  • If blocked, approach the Circle Office of the Food Department with your original Aadhaar and Ration Card to undergo manual re-activation through a fresh e-KYC process.

The transition to a "digital-only" application system has created barriers for the poor and elderly who are not digitally literate, requiring them to rely on middlemen or navigate the e-District portal.

How to fix:

  • Avoid unofficial middlemen. Visit a government-authorized 'Doorstep Delivery' facilitation center or call the helpline number 1076 to request assistance with digital formalities at your home.
  • You can also visit the nearest Circle Office where 'help-desks' are legally required to assist citizens with online entries.

Complaints persist regarding ration shop dealers misusing PoS machines, such as claiming the machine failed while recording a successful transaction in their records, or demanding bribes.

How to fix:

  • Always insist on a printed receipt for every transaction. If the dealer claims a technical failure, verify your transaction status on the NFS portal in real-time.
  • Lodge a complaint via the PGMS Delhi portal or call the department helpline at 1967.

Homeless or migrant populations often face issues in obtaining or updating ration cards due to lack of local address proof, even when having an Aadhaar card.

How to fix:

  • Utilize the 'One Nation One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme. If you have an active ration card in your home state, you can collect your share of ration from any FPS in Delhi using only your Aadhaar and biometric verification.
  • Migrants do not need a fresh Delhi address proof to collect 'portable' ration entitlement.
